title: Add working location search to QissaMap with story pins

requirements.summary: Add visible search input bar to QissaMap page allowing users to search locations using Nominatim geocoding API
acceptanceCriteria: A search input field is visible on the QissaMap page, positioned prominently (e.g., top-center overlay on the map).
User can type a location name and submit (Enter key or search button).
The Nominatim API is called with the entered query to resolve coordinates.
If no results are found, a user-friendly 'No location found' message is shown.

file_operations.description: Add state management for search query, search results, and selected search location. Import and integrate the search input component overlay. Handle search submission by calling Nominatim API (reuse the geocoding logic from MapView.tsx). Display error toast when no location is found. Pass the searched location coordinates to QissaMapView for map panning and marker placement.

Create a new search bar component that renders a text input with a search icon/button, positioned as an absolute overlay at the top-center of the map. Accept props for onChange, onSearch callbacks, and current search value. Include Enter key handler for submission. Style with semi-transparent background, rounded corners, and shadow for visibility over the map. Ensure mobile-responsive sizing.

Accept new optional prop `searchedLocation` containing coordinates and location name. When searchedLocation changes, programmatically pan/fly the map to those coordinates using Leaflet's flyTo method with smooth animation. Ensure the map re-centers appropriately when a new search is performed.

requirements.summary: Pan map to searched location and place a distinct pin marker at the searched coordinates
acceptanceCriteria: The map smoothly pans/zooms to the searched location's coordinates.
A distinct marker (different style from story markers) is placed at the searched location.
The pin remains visible as long as the search result is active.
Clearing the search input removes the location pin.

file_operations.description: Add state to track the searched location marker. When searchedLocation prop is present, create a Leaflet marker with a distinct icon (e.g., a search pin icon or a different colored marker) at the searched coordinates. Use Leaflet's marker API to add this marker to the map. When searchedLocation is cleared (null/undefined), remove the marker from the map. Ensure the marker is visually distinct from story cluster markers and the user location marker.

Create a factory function that returns a Leaflet divIcon for the searched location marker. Style it distinctly from the current-location marker (frontend/src/lib/currentLocationMarker.ts) using a different color (e.g., red or blue) and shape (e.g., a pin icon or a larger circle with a label). Export the factory function for use in QissaMapView.

Add a clear button or X icon inside the search bar that resets the search query and clears the searchedLocation state when clicked. Ensure that clearing the search removes the searched location marker from the map.

requirements.summary: Fetch and display popular stories near the searched location as floating story cards or labeled markers on the map
acceptanceCriteria: Upon successful location search, the app queries for stories near those coordinates.
Stories are ranked/filtered by popularity (likes or pins count).
Up to 5–10 popular stories are displayed as floating cards or enriched markers on the map near the pinned location.
Each floating story indicator shows the story title and a brief excerpt or category badge.
Clicking a floating story opens the StoryDetailDialog for that story.

file_operations.description: After a successful search, trigger a backend query using the existing getNearbyStoriesForMap hook with the searched location's coordinates and a fixed radius (e.g., 10–20 km). Sort the returned stories by popularity (pinCount or likeCount) and take the top 5–10 stories. Pass this filtered array of popular stories to QissaMapView for rendering as enriched markers or floating cards.

Accept a new optional prop `popularStories` containing an array of Story objects. When popularStories is provided, render each story as a marker with a popup or custom overlay. Use Leaflet popups or divIcons to display the story title and a brief excerpt (first 50 characters) or category badge. Ensure these markers are visually distinct from the searched location marker and regular story cluster markers (e.g., use a star icon or a colored pin). Attach click handlers to each popular story marker that open the StoryDetailDialog for that story.

Create a factory function that returns a Leaflet divIcon for popular story markers. Style it with a distinctive icon (e.g., a star or a highlighted pin) and include a small label or badge showing the story's category emoji. Export the factory function for use in QissaMapView.
